Name: Annual Mean Temperature
Definition: Mean of the 12 monthly mean temperatures

Name: Mean Diurnal Range
Definition: Mean of monthly (max temp - min temp)

Name: Temperature Seasonality
Definition: Standard deviation of monthly mean temperatures

Name: Mean Temperature of Wettest Quarter
Definition: Mean temperature of the wettest 3 consecutive months

Name: Mean Temperature of Driest Quarter
Definition: Mean temperature of the driest 3 consecutive months

Name: Mean Temperature of Warmest Quarter
Definition: Mean temperature of the warmest 3 consecutive months

Name: Mean Temperature of Coldest Quarter
Definition: Mean temperature of the coldest 3 consecutive months

Name: Annual Precipitation
Definition: Sum of the 12 monthly precipitation totals
Notes: kg->L conversion performed using the water-density identity (1 kg/m^2 ≈ 1 L/m^2 ≈ 1 mm, density ~1000 kg/m^3); WorldClim's own BioClim layers report this in mm; we prefer CHELSA's dimensionally-equivalent kg*m^-2 form here since a single entry serves both sources

Name: Precipitation of Wettest Month
Definition: Precipitation of the month with the highest total
Notes: kg->L conversion performed using the water-density identity (1 kg/m^2 ≈ 1 L/m^2 ≈ 1 mm, density ~1000 kg/m^3); WorldClim's own BioClim layers report this in mm; we prefer CHELSA's dimensionally-equivalent kg*m^-2 form here since a single entry serves both sources

Name: Precipitation of Driest Month
Definition: Precipitation of the month with the lowest total
Notes: kg->L conversion performed using the water-density identity (1 kg/m^2 ≈ 1 L/m^2 ≈ 1 mm, density ~1000 kg/m^3); WorldClim's own BioClim layers report this in mm; we prefer CHELSA's dimensionally-equivalent kg*m^-2 form here since a single entry serves both sources

Name: Precipitation Seasonality
Definition: Coefficient of variation of monthly precipitation

Name: Precipitation of Wettest Quarter
Definition: Total precipitation of the wettest 3 consecutive months
Notes: kg->L conversion performed using the water-density identity (1 kg/m^2 ≈ 1 L/m^2 ≈ 1 mm, density ~1000 kg/m^3); WorldClim's own BioClim layers report this in mm; we prefer CHELSA's dimensionally-equivalent kg*m^-2 form here since a single entry serves both sources
